Organic Inputs Sub‑index

Source: vignettes/articles/OrgInputs.Rmd
OrgInputs.Rmd

Amendments + Animals (Weighted Annual Frequency)

Overview

The SHMI Organic Inputs subindex quantifies the frequency of biological inputs—organic amendments and animal presence—across the rotation. Each rotation year is assigned a weighted organic‑input presence based on user‑specified weights for amendments and animals. The final score is scaled to 0–100, where higher values indicate more frequent biological inputs.

This sub‑index captures management practices that add organic matter, stimulate microbial activity, and contribute to soil biological functioning.

1. Rotation‑Year Grid

For each management unit ii, a sequence of rotation years is constructed:

year∈{rot_start_yri,…,rot_end_yri} \text{year} \in \{\text{rot_start_yr}_i, \ldots, \text{rot_end_yr}_i\}

This defines the evaluation window for organic inputs.

2. Amendment Events

Organic amendments are identified from the amendment table:

  • Only rows with SA_cat="Organic"\text{SA_cat} = \text{"Organic"} contribute.
  • Amendment years are extracted as:

year=year(SA_date) \text{year} = \text{year}(\text{SA_date})

Each amendment year is marked:

amend_presenti,y=1 \text{amend_present}_{i,y} = 1

All other years receive 0.

3. Animal Events

Animal presence is identified from the animal table:

year=year(AD_start_date) \text{year} = \text{year}(\text{AD_start_date})

Each year with animal presence is marked:

ani_presenti,y=1 \text{ani_present}_{i,y} = 1

All other years receive 0.

4. Weighted Organic‑Input Presence

For each rotation year:

weighted_inputi,y=wamend⋅amend_presenti,y+wanimal⋅ani_presenti,y \text{weighted\_input}_{i,y} = w_{\text{amend}} \cdot \text{amend_present}_{i,y} + w_{\text{animal}} \cdot \text{ani_present}_{i,y}

Default weights:

  • wamend=1w_{\text{amend}} = 1
  • wanimal=1w_{\text{animal}} = 1

Weights allow tuning based on local priorities or empirical optimization.

5. Rotation‑Average Input Frequency

Let YiY_i be the number of rotation years for management unit ii.
Total weighted inputs across the rotation:

total_weightedi=∑yweighted_inputi,y \text{total_weighted}_i = \sum_{y} \text{weighted_input}_{i,y}

Annualized frequency:

events_per_yeari=total_weightediYi \text{events_per_year}_i = \frac{\text{total_weighted}_i}{Y_i}

If Yi=0Y_i = 0, the value defaults to 0.

6. Scaling to 0–100

If all management units have zero events:

OrgInputsi=0 \text{OrgInputs}_i = 0

Otherwise, values are rescaled to [0,100][0,100] using a min–max transformation:

OrgInputsi=100×events_per_yeari−min(events_per_year)max(events_per_year)−min(events_per_year) \text{OrgInputs}_i = 100 \times \frac{\text{events_per_year}_i - \min(\text{events_per_year})} {\max(\text{events_per_year}) - \min(\text{events_per_year})}

This ensures comparability across systems with different event frequencies.

Interpretation

  • 0 → No organic amendments or animal presence across the rotation
  • 100 → Highest observed frequency of biological inputs
  • Weights allow emphasizing amendments, animals, or both
  • Captures management intensity related to organic matter and biological activity

Output

A data frame with one row per management unit:

MGT_combo OrgInputs
… 0–100

On this page
